title: Group Travel in Kagurazaka: An 80㎡ House Steps from Tokyo's 'Little Paris'
excerpt: Why Plat House Kagurazaka's spacious 1LDK house, just 1 minute from the station, is built for groups of up to 10.
contentHtml:
Kagurazaka earned its nickname 'Little Paris' honestly — cobblestone alleys, French bistros tucked behind old wooden facades, and a slower pace than most of central Tokyo. It's an unusual blend of Edo-era Japan and French influence, and it makes for one of the most charming bases in the city.

80㎡, One Minute from the Station

Plat House Kagurazaka is a full 1LDK house sleeping up to 10 guests, just a 1-minute walk from Kagurazaka Station. Instead of splitting a large group across multiple hotel rooms, everyone shares one address — with a proper kitchen, living room, and laundry facilities that make it feel like a home rather than a rental.

Comfort for Big Groups

Netflix and a Nintendo Switch keep the group entertained, and the kitchen is stocked for real cooking, not just reheating. With laundry facilities on-site, longer stays with a full group don't turn into a logistics problem. Self check-in also means no coordinating arrival times around a front desk for a group this size.

Cobblestone Streets and Century-Old Shrines

Kagurazaka's stone-paved back alleys are worth an evening walk on their own, and Bishamonten Zenkokuji Temple and Akagi Shrine are both close by for a quieter side of the neighborhood. Waseda University's historic campus is a short trip away if your group wants to see more of the area.

Book Direct and Save

Booking directly with Plat House Kagurazaka beats typical OTA pricing, with free cancellation up to 7 days before check-in.
